how.title: How this verification works
how.p1: The provenance and authenticity of this content is recorded on the Solana blockchain via Title Protocol.
how.p2: At capture time, a C2PA signature is generated inside the device's TEE (Trusted Execution Environment), and its hash is recorded on-chain. Even without the original C2PA data, authenticity can be verified trustlessly by checking the on-chain record and TEE signature.
how.p3: The perceptual hash (pHash) is computed by a WASM module inside the TEE, and compared against a value recomputed from the displayed image. This verifies that the displayed image matches the on-chain record — without needing the original file.
how.p4: All verification data is fetched directly from Solana RPC and Arweave. No RootLens server is involved in the verification process. You can confirm this in your browser's network inspector.
how.tsa: The timestamp is recorded by an RFC 3161-compliant TSA (Time Stamping Authority), providing third-party assurance of the capture time.
